author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The days have come for us to sing Allelujah.…Now these days come only to pass away, and pass away to come, again, and typify the day which does not come and pass away, because it is neither preceded by yesterday to cause it to come, nor pressed upon by the morrow to cause it to pass.…For as these days succeed in regular season, with a joyful cheerfulness, the past days of Lent, whereby the misery of this life before the Resurrection of the Lord’s body is signified; so that day which after the Resurrection shall be given to the full body of the Lord, that is, to the holy Church, when all the troubles and sorrows of this life have been shut out, shall succeed with perpetual bliss.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this life demandeth from us self-restraint, that although groaning and weighed down with our toil and struggles, and desiring to be clothed upon with our house which is from heaven, we may refrain from secular pleasures: and this is signified by the number of forty, which was the period of the fasts of Moses, and Elias, and our Lord Himself.…But by the number fifty after our Lord’s resurrection, during which season we sing Allelujah, not the term and passing away of a certain season is signified, but that blessed eternity; because the denary added to forty signifieth the reward paid to the faithful who toil in this life, which our Father hath prepared an equal share of for the first and for the last.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore hear the heart of the people of God full of divine praises.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He representeth in this Psalm some one exulting in happy joyfulness, he prefigureth the people whose hearts are overflowing with the love of God, that is, the body of Christ, freed from all evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will make confession unto Thee, O Lord,” he saith, “with my whole heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Confession is not always confession of sins, but the praise of God is poured forth in the devotion of confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The former mourneth, the latter rejoiceth: the former showeth the wound to the physician, the latter giveth thanks for health.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The latter confession signifieth some one, not merely freed from every evil, but even separate from all the ill-disposed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And for this reason let us consider the place where he confesseth unto the Lord with all his heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the counsel,” he saith, “of the upright, and in the congregation:” I suppose, of those who shall “sit upon the twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there will be no longer an unjust man among them, the thefts of no Judas are allowed, no Simon Magus is baptized, wishing to buy the Spirit, whilst he designeth to sell it; no coppersmith like Alexander doth many evil deeds, no man covered with sheep’s clothing creepeth in with feigned fraternity; such as those among whom the Church must now groan, and such as she must then shut out, when all the righteous shall be gathered together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “These are the great works of the Lord, sought out unto all His wills” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2): through which mercy forsaketh none who confesseth, no man’s wickedness is unpunished.…Let man choose for himself what he listeth: the works of the Lord are not so constituted, that the creature, having free discretion allowed him, should transcend the will of the Creator, even though he act contrary to His will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God willeth not that thou shouldest sin; for He forbiddeth it: yet if thou hast sinned, imagine not that the man hath done what he willed, and that hath happened to God which He willed not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as He would that man would not sin, so would He spare the sinner, that he may return and live; He so willeth finally to punish him who persisteth in his sin, that the rebellious cannot escape the power of justice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus whatever choice thou hast made, the Almighty will not be at a loss to fulfil His will concerning thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Confession and glorious deeds are His work” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is a more glorious deed than to justify the ungodly?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps the work of man preventeth that glorious work of God, so that when he hath confessed his sins, he deserveth to be justified.…This is the glorious work of the Lord: for he loveth most, to whom most is forgiven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the glorious work of the Lord: for “where sin abounded, there did grace much more abound.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps a man would deserve justification from works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Not,” saith he, “of works, lest any man boast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a man worketh not righteousness save he be justified: but by “believing on Him that justifieth the ungodly,” he beginneth with faith; that good may not by preceding show what he hath deserved, but by following what he hath received.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath made His wonderful works to be remembered” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4): by abasing this man, exalting that.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Reserving unusual miracles for a fit season, that thus human weakness, intent upon novelty, may remember them, although His daily miracles be greater.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He created so many trees throughout the whole earth, and no one wondereth: He dried up one with a word, and the hearts of mortals were thunderstruck.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that miracle, which hath not through its frequency become common, will cling most firmly to the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But of what use were the miracles, save that He might be feared?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What too would fear profit, unless “the gracious and merciful Lord” gave “meat unto them that fear Him”? (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5); meat that doth not spoil, “bread that cometh down from heaven,” which He gave to no deservings of ours.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “Christ died for the ungodly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one then would give such food, save a gracious and merciful Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if He gave so much to this life, if the sinner who was to be justified received the Word made flesh; what shall he receive when glorified in a future world?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “He shall ever be mindful of His covenant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor hath He who hath given a pledge, given the whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall show His people the power of His works” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not the holy Israelites, who have left all their possessions and have followed Him, be saddened; let them not be sorrowful and say, “Who then can be saved?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “with men these things are impossible, but with God all things are possible.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That He may give them the heritage of the heathen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they went to the heathen, and enjoined the rich of this world “not to be high-minded, nor to trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God,” to whom that is easy which is difficult for men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus many were called, thus the heritage of the heathen has been occupied, thus it hath happened, that even many who have not abandoned all their possessions in this life in order to follow Him, have despised even life itself for the sake of confessing His Name; and like camels humbling themselves to bear the burden of troubles, have entered as it were through a needle’s eye, through the piercing straits of suffering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath wrought these effects, unto whom all things are possible.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The works of His hands are verity and judgment” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let verity be held by those who are judged here.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Martyrs are here sentenced, and brought to the judgment-seat, that they may judge not only those by whom they have been judged, but even give judgment on angels, against whom was their struggle here, even when they seemed to be judged by men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not tribulation, distress, famine, nakedness, the sword, separate from Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “all His commandments are true;” He deceiveth not, He giveth us what He promised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet we should not expect here what He promised; we should not hope for it: but “they stand fast for ever and ever, and are done in truth and equity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is equitable and just that we should labour here and repose there; since “He sent redemption unto His people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But from what are they redeemed, save from the captivity of this pilgrimage?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not therefore rest be sought, save in the heavenly country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God indeed gave the carnal Israelites an earthly Jerusalem, “which is in bondage with her children:” but this is the Old Covenant, pertaining unto the old man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they who there understood the figure, even then were heirs of the New Covenant; for “Jerusalem which is above is free, which is our everlasting mother in heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that transitory promises were given in that Old Testament is proved by the fact itself: however, “He hath commended His covenant for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what, but the New?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whosoever dost wish to be heir of this, deceive not thyself, and think not of a land flowing with milk and honey, nor of pleasant farms, nor of gardens abounding in fruits and shade: desire not how to gain anything of this sort, such as the eye of covetousness is wont to lust for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For since “covetousness is the root of all evils,” it must be cut off, that it may be consumed here; not be put off, that it may be satisfied there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First escape punishments, avoid hell; before thou longest for a God who promiseth, beware of one who threateneth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “holy and reverend is His Name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7. …“The fear of the Lord,” therefore, “is the beginning of wisdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Understanding is good” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who gainsayeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to understand, and not to do, is dangerous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is “good,” therefore, “to those that do thereafter.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor let it lift up the mind unto pride; for, “the praise of Him,” the fear of whom is the beginning of wisdom, “endureth for ever:” and this will be the reward, this the end, this the everlasting station and abode.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are found the true commandments, made fast for ever and ever; here is the very heritage of the New Covenant commanded for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “One thing,” he saith, “I have desired of the Lord, which I will require: even that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “blessed are they that dwell in the house” of the Lord: “they will be alway praising” Him; for “His praise endureth for ever.”
